residual_risks: Milestone-5 through milestone-7 remain intentionally open and rows 5-7 stay NO in the mechanism table.

The accepted result freezes the current private lowerer-owned layout policy rather than evaluating alternate runtime layouts, so any later representation redesign will need new evidence and a new accepted round.
evidence_summary: Reviewed the canonical round-225 worktree against master commit 2c1661b3a21fea0065c6f68c57bac2d5b2585f23, where HEAD, master, and merge-base all match and the live round exists as an uncommitted worktree diff. The implementation-owned merge payload stays limited to docs/architecture.md, docs/backend-native-pipeline.md, src/MLF/Backend/IR.hs, src/MLF/Backend/Convert.hs, src/MLF/Backend/LLVM/Lower.hs, docs/plans/2026-05-02-backend-ir-executable-boundary-mechanism-table.md, test/BackendLLVMSpec.hs, and test/RepoGuardSpec.hs, while orchestrator/state.json, the pointer stubs, the active rev-001 roadmap bundle, and the round-225 lineage/review files are controller-owned activation and review bookkeeping. The round makes the ADT/case semantic-versus-layout ownership split explicit across the synchronized contract surfaces, keeps runtime tag values, field layout, closure-field storage, and nullary representation private to MLF.Backend.LLVM.Lower, flips only mechanism-table row 4 to YES while preserving rows 1-3 at YES and rows 5-7 at NO, passes all focused row-4 slices, and passes cabal build all && cabal test with 2341 examples and 0 failures. The inherited one-backend-IR, eager-runtime, no-lazy-STG, and no-public-lower-IR boundaries remain intact, and the round does not widen into milestone-5/6/7 claims, fallback runtime paths, or a second lowering surface.
